results.question: What are the components of Central Government debt as defined under the FRBM Act?
results.expected_summary: As per Section 2(aa) of the FRBM Act (amended in 2018), Central Government Debt at any date includes: (1) Total outstanding liabilities on the security of the Consolidated Fund of India, including external debt valued at current exchange rate, (2) Total outstanding liabilities in the Public Account of India, (3) The financial liabilities of any body corporate or other entity owned or controlled by the Central Government which the Government is to repay or service from the Annual Financial Statement, and (4) Cash Balance at the end of that date (which is deducted).
results.actual_answer: The components of Central Government debt as defined under the FRBM Act, amended in 2018, include the components of Extra Budgetary Resources (EBR) and cash balance [Chapter 2: FRBM Targets and Achievements > 2.1 Central Government Debt and General Government Debt > 2.1.1 Outstanding Central Government Debt, p.20-21].
